title: Kalanchoe porphyrocalyx
taxonomy: Kalanchoe porphyrocalyx is classified within the family Crassulaceae. This succulent plant belongs to the genus Kalanchoe, which comprises over 125 species. Kalanchoe porphyrocalyx is native to Madagascar, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to the region's climate. The Crassulaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as stonecrops or orpine, and Kalanchoe porphyrocalyx is a notable member of this family. By classifying Kalanchoe porphyrocalyx within the Crassulaceae family, botanists can better understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this group.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
